question: How is SCF funding distributed across ecosystem categories — which categories get the most?

golden.answer:
A grounded answer derives its named examples and status claims from the live Stellar Scout directory or research data, then dates the roster so it is not presented as permanent. As of 2026-07-10, the verified sources supported this observed answer:

Use Stellar Light's **`/api/analyze?dimension=categories`** for a corpus-relative aggregate, not per-project history or the currently truncated clusters endpoint. Label output per the Scout corpus with `generatedAt`, taxonomy, project/status scope, and methodology. As of **2026-07-10**, the active-project basis had 841 projects and 391 funded projects totaling a reconstructed **$39,851,645.13**. **User-Facing App** led total projects (337), funded projects (167), and reconstructed dollars (**$16.904M / 42.42%**). Dollar order was User-Facing App > Infrastructure ($7.809M) > Protocol/Contract ($7.693M) > Tooling ($6.249M). **Protocol/Contract** separately had the highest funded rate, 68/129 = 52.71%. These are dated Scout-service metrics, not official SCF category accounting; re-query rather than freezing them.

The dated example is illustrative: a later grounded answer should report what the same source basis supports at its own observation time while preserving the stable distinctions above.
keyFacts: Presents specific source-supported content as a current or dated observation rather than as an unsupported memory claim.
Makes the as-of date visible for every changeable roster, status, version, or measurement.
Reports category funding using an explicit category and amount basis.
avoid: Do NOT present Scout category counts/dollar estimates as independently official SCF totals or permanent truth.
Do NOT fabricate percentages/splits or answer with per-project history when category distribution was requested.
Do NOT present the observed live result set as a complete or permanent roster; explicitly historical facts remain historical.
golden.notes:
2nd scout_analyze case; exercises the categories dimension ('projects per category'). Refreshed 2026-06-29 via /api/analyze?dimension=categories: User-Facing App 204/103/$9.19M, Tooling 108/42/$3.28M, Infrastructure 97/34/$3.01M, Protocol/Contract 86/44/$4.48M (totalProjects 500). Live dollar figures → flipped freshness_sensitive true; ranking headline (UFA leads count+dollars, then Tooling/Infra/Protocol) unchanged.
Also good if the answer: Frames it as an aggregate distribution, not a per-project breakdown.
Golden cites: Stellar Light analytics (scout_analyze categories/funding dimension).
GT-37 REFRESH 2026-07-10: use /api/analyze as the explicit basis. UFA leads absolute count and reconstructed dollars; Protocol/Contract leads funded rate. /api/clusters truncates at 500 and must not supply this denominator until fixed. Exact values remain dated corpus analytics.
